pattern: Track how many people newly learn the secret on each day, then a sliding window of the still-sharing days feeds the next day's new learners, with day-n knowers being everyone whose learning day has not yet reached the forget horizon.
hints: Let f[d] be the number of people who first learn the secret on day d; only these counts matter, not individuals.
On day d the new learners come from everyone who learned it between d-forget+1 and d-delay, since those are exactly the people currently able to share.
Maintain a running sum of the active sharers' counts as the window slides, all under the modulus, and finally sum the counts that have not yet been forgotten.

edge_inputs.description: share next day and forget quickly
smallest n with delay just under forget
delay and forget span the whole horizon
people forget on the same day they would first share
long forget keeps everyone counted, exercising growth
large n stresses the modular reduction

stress.description: random valid (n, delay, forget) with 1 <= delay < forget <= n
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 1000)))
    forget = rng.randint(2, n)
    delay = rng.randint(1, forget - 1)
    return [n, delay, forget]
